name: Kealia Trail
tagline: The best-kept-secret ridge trail on the North Shore — wide open views from Dillingham to the Waianae Mountains.
parking: Free dirt pulloff on Farrington Highway past Dillingham Airfield — look for the trail marker on the mauka side
bestTime: Early morning — zero shade on this trail so you'll bake by 10am
talkStory: The trail follows an old military access road built to service a radar installation on the Waianae Range summit during the Cold War — you can still see concrete pads at the top. The name Kealia means 'the salt-encrusted place,' referencing ancient Hawaiian salt pans near the coast below. As you climb, you'll look down on Dillingham Airfield where skydivers free-fall beneath you — a surreal perspective. It connects to Kuaokala Trail at the top, and the combined through-hike is one of Oahu's most ambitious day trips.
